content: 2024-2025 state-of-the-art active learning agent memory systems can be re-framed as hierarchical collective agents, where every individual memory entry acts as an autonomous curiosity-driven micro-agent that actively bids to be included in the working memory context based on its own intrinsic reward for reducing the core agent’s current uncertainty. This design reframes memory retrieval from a static similarity-based lookup to an active, collective selection process that inherently aligns with the core active learning objective.
